subject: Accounting
title: The Highest-Taxed Countries
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Highest-Taxed Countries.

hook.text: Denmark's top income tax rate is about 55%. Its citizens rank among the happiest on Earth.

buildup.text: Scandinavian countries collect more tax than almost anywhere—funding universal healthcare and education.

discovery.text: In return, citizens pay little for college, childcare, or medical care. The trade-off is visible.

twist.text: Despite high taxes, these countries rank high for business competitiveness and innovation.

climax.text: High tax doesn't automatically mean poor economy—it depends on how the money is spent.

punchline.text: High taxes, high trust, high happiness. It's a package deal.

quiz.question: What do Scandinavian countries provide in exchange for high taxes?
options: Universal healthcare, education, and social services
